title_zh: 在AI峰会上，韩国与NVIDIA及合作伙伴描绘AI未来
title_en: At AI Summit, South Korea Outlines Its AI Future With NVIDIA and Partners
summary_zh: 本周在旧金山举行的AI峰会上，韩国总统李在明与该国顶尖企业领导者及研究人员，与NVIDIA及生态合作伙伴会面，规划韩国的AI发展。继NVIDIA创始人兼CEO黄仁勋上月访韩之后，会议中的讨论和公告推动了韩国AI的进步。
summary_en: At this week’s AI Summit in San Francisco, South Korean President Jae Myung Lee, along with leading business leaders and researchers, met with NVIDIA and ecosystem partners to map out Korea’s AI progress. Building on NVIDIA founder and CEO Jensen Huang’s visit to Korea last month, discussions and announcements this week advance Korea’s AI efforts.
why_it_matters_zh: 这些合作和交流展现出韩国在全球AI领域的积极布局和创新决心，标志着韩国致力于利用AI技术推动经济和产业升级。
why_it_matters_en: These collaborations and exchanges demonstrate South Korea’s proactive stance and commitment to innovation in the global AI arena, signaling its goal to leverage AI for economic and industrial advancement.

why_it_matters_zh: 该实验室的成立将促进韩国内自主智能体AI技术的研发与应用，推动韩国的人工智能创新与产业发展。
why_it_matters_en: The establishment of this lab aims to boost research and application of agentic AI within South Korea, advancing AI innovation and industrial growth in the country.

title_zh: 用于参数化CAD的自监督学习掩码拓扑建模
title_en: Masked Topology Modeling for Self-Supervised Learning on Parametric CAD
summary_zh: 计算机辅助设计（CAD）无处不在：几乎所有现代物体均使用可编辑的CAD工具设计。然而，缺乏以其原生可编辑和参数化格式（边界表示，B-Rep）存在的CAD数据集，促使开发高效数据利用方法显得尤为重要。本文提出一种新的自监督预训练任务——掩码拓扑建模（MTM），利用B-Rep独有的面邻接图结构，要求编码器重构。MTM随机掩码部分边，并训练小型头部从编码器的面特征预测被掩边的凸性和曲线类型。结合基于MoCo风格动量队列的对比学习、B-Rep感知增强、一种基于广度优先搜索的面区域掩码重构目标，以及在ABC数据集和新建程序生成数据集上的预训练，展示了在多个基准上的强劲性能。
summary_en: Computer aided design (CAD) is ubiquitous, with most modern objects designed using editable parametric CAD tools. Due to the shortage of available CAD datasets in their native editable boundary representation (B-Rep) format, it is critical to develop data-efficient methods. This work introduces Masked Topology Modeling (MTM), a self-supervised pretraining task that leverages the unique face-adjacency graph structure of B-Reps to train an encoder to reconstruct masked edges. MTM masks some edges and trains a small head to predict their convexity and curve type from face features. Combined with MoCo-style momentum-queue contrastive learning, B-Rep-aware augmentations, a BFS-connected masked face-region reconstruction objective, and pretraining on both ABC and a new procedurally generated dataset, the approach achieves strong benchmark results.
why_it_matters_zh: 这项工作提出了一种针对参数化CAD数据的创新自监督方法，利用了B-Rep独特的拓扑结构，显著提高了有限数据条件下的预训练效果，为CAD智能设计提供了新的技术路径。
why_it_matters_en: This work introduces an innovative self-supervised approach tailored to parametric CAD data by exploiting unique B-Rep topology, significantly enhancing pretraining efficacy under limited data and offering a new technical avenue for intelligent CAD design.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: Masked Topology Modeling for Self-Supervised Learning on Parametric CAD. Category: coding_ai. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.

title_zh: Axolotl3D：一个统一的真实3D形状补全框架
title_en: Axolotl3D: a Unified Framework for Faithful 3D Shape Completion
summary_zh: 当前的3D生成模型依赖大规模先验和扩散架构，从单张图像生成高质量几何形状，但假设物体完全可见且仅用单视角输入，限制了其在多视角、遮挡或编辑场景中的应用。Axolotl3D提出一个多模态且具备遮挡感知的3D生成模型，联合利用图像、可见性掩模、相机参数和局部点云进行条件控制。点云作为几何锚点促进准确形状补全，相机参数确保多视角在统一3D坐标系下的对齐。其统一训练策略通过大规模3D数据合成多样化条件，增强跨模态推理能力。实验表明，Axolotl3D在清晰及被遮挡条件下均达到先进性能，并在真实世界重建和几何一致编辑任务中表现优异。
summary_en: Recent 3D generative models produce high-quality geometry from single images using large-scale priors and diffusion architectures but assume complete visibility and single-view inputs, limiting their use in multi-view, occluded, or editing scenarios. Axolotl3D introduces a multi-modal, occlusion-aware 3D generation model jointly conditioned on images, visibility masks, camera parameters, and partial point clouds. The point cloud acts as a geometric anchor for faithful shape completion, while camera parameters ensure consistent multi-view alignment in a shared 3D coordinate system. A unified training strategy synthesizes diverse conditioning regimes from large-scale 3D data, enabling robust cross-modal reasoning. Experiments on Toys4K and OmniObject3D datasets show state-of-the-art performance in both clean and occluded settings, as well as strong results in real-world reconstruction and geometry-consistent editing.
why_it_matters_zh: Axolotl3D通过整合多种条件信号，实现了在复杂遮挡和多视角情况下的精准3D形状补全，突破了以往单视角模型的局限，提升了三维重建和编辑的实际应用价值。
why_it_matters_en: By integrating multiple conditioning signals, Axolotl3D enables accurate 3D shape completion even under occlusions and multi-view settings, overcoming limitations of single-view models and enhancing practical applications in 3D reconstruction and editing.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: Axolotl3D: a Unified Framework for Faithful 3D Shape Completion. Category: image_ai. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.
ranking_reason: Selected from authoritative primary source; quality score 65.

title_zh: ODeform：基于神经常微分方程学习连续4D运动进行形状变形
title_en: ODeform: Learning Continuous 4D Motion for Shape Deformation with Neural ODEs
summary_zh: 连续物体形变建模对于计算机视觉和机器人任务（如操作和仿真）非常重要。现有方法依赖基于学习的方法或物理模拟器，但要么采用离散时间步长，要么计算复杂度高，不适合实时应用。ODeform提出将神经常微分方程扩展到三维空间中可形变物体的连续4D动力学，通过将3D点云和物理条件映射到统一潜空间，利用ODE连续建模形变运动，提升了计算效率。实验证明其在未见物理参数配置下的运动预测准确性优于基线方法，并能成功迁移到真实3D对象，实现了动力学的有效插值与外推。相关代码与数据将公开。
summary_en: Modeling continuous object deformation is crucial for tasks in computer vision and robotics like manipulation and simulation. Existing methods often use discrete time steps or heavy physics simulators, limiting real-time use. ODeform extends Neural Ordinary Differential Equations to continuous 4D dynamics of deformable 3D objects by embedding 3D point clouds and physical properties into a unified latent space, then solving ODEs to model continuous deformation flows efficiently. Evaluations show improved motion prediction on unseen physical parameters over baselines, successful transfer to real 3D captured objects with novel shapes, and effective interpolation and extrapolation of learned dynamics. The code and data will be publicly released.
why_it_matters_zh: ODeform突破了传统离散时间步长模型的限制，实现了对连续4D形变动力学的高效建模，提升了运动预测准确性和泛化能力，且适用于真实3D物体的迁移，推动计算机视觉与机器人领域的实时形变仿真技术发展。
why_it_matters_en: ODeform overcomes limitations of discrete time-step models by enabling efficient continuous 4D deformation dynamics modeling, improving motion prediction accuracy and generalization, and enabling transfer to real 3D objects. This advances real-time shape deformation simulation in computer vision and robotics.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: ODeform: Learning Continuous 4D Motion for Shape Deformation with Neural ODEs. Category: coding_ai. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.

title_zh: 标记错误的症状：评估医学文本中大型语言模型的水印效果
title_en: Marking the Wrong Symptoms: Evaluating LLM Watermarks in Medical Texts
summary_zh: 本文首次严格研究了大型语言模型（LLM）水印在医疗领域的表现，测试了5种水印方案，涵盖11个LLM和7个视觉语言模型（VLM），评估了单模态和多模态临床推理任务。通过引入专家验证的审计流程，分析了水印对医学推理质量、术语精确度和虚假信息的影响。结果显示，水印会导致词汇污染、虚假术语、图像发现误归因或遗漏等多种严重表现下降。研究强调缺乏领域特定评估和依赖总体指标可能掩盖医疗文本中的实际问题。
summary_en: This work presents the first rigorous study of how watermarks in large language models (LLMs) affect medical performance. It benchmarks 5 watermarking schemes across 11 LLMs and 7 vision-language models (VLMs) on unimodal and multimodal clinical reasoning tasks. Introducing a human-expert validated pipeline, the study audits medical reasoning quality, terminological precision, and hallucinations. Results reveal watermarks induce significant degradation including lexical corruption, hallucinated terminology, and misattribution or omission of image findings. The study highlights that lacking domain-specific analysis and relying on aggregate metrics can obscure critical clinical failures.
why_it_matters_zh: 医疗文本对准确性要求极高，水印带来的微小改变可能导致严重的误诊或遗漏，研究指出必须进行领域特定评估，确保水印技术在临床应用中安全可靠。
why_it_matters_en: Medical texts require high precision where minor perturbations can cause serious errors. The study stresses the necessity of domain-specific evaluation to ensure watermarking techniques do not compromise clinical safety and reliability.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: Marking the Wrong Symptoms: Evaluating LLM Watermarks in Medical Texts. Category: image_ai. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.
ranking_reason: Selected from authoritative primary source; quality score 71.

title_zh: 语言模型的期望对齐以满足真实用户期望
title_en: Expectation Alignment of Language Models for Real-World User Expectations
summary_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）在标准基准测试中表现出色，但是否真正满足用户期望尚未充分探索。现有评估方法依赖模型启发、专家评分或用户模拟，难以捕捉真实人类期望的多样性与细微差别，导致模型看似合格却与用户实际需求不符。本文首次系统研究真实用户期望，提出提取语义丰富期望的原则方法，并引入基于真实用户期望的ExpectBench基准。分析显示当前模型难以满足和预判用户期望，体现根本性错位。为此，作者提出轻量潜在期望感知响应生成框架LENS，使模型内化用户期望，提升对齐度和期望满足，强调明确建模用户期望对于人机现实对齐的重要性。
summary_en: Large language models (LLMs) perform well on benchmarks but often fail to meet diverse and subtle real human expectations. Existing evaluations relying on heuristics, expert rubrics, or simulations do not fully capture these expectations, causing misalignment. This work conducts the first systematic study of user expectations in real-world LLM usage, proposes a principled method to extract semantically rich expectations, and introduces ExpectBench, a benchmark grounded in real user expectations. The study reveals that current LLMs struggle to satisfy and anticipate user hopes, highlighting fundamental misalignment. To address this, the authors propose LENS, a lightweight latent expectation-aware response generation framework that enables models to internalize user expectations and generate more aligned responses, consistently improving expectation satisfaction and underscoring the importance of explicitly modeling user expectations for realistic human-AI alignment.
why_it_matters_zh: 这一研究揭示了目前大型语言模型与真实用户期望之间的本质错位，填补了用户期望系统性分析的空白。提出的ExpectBench基准和LENS框架为提升语言模型的用户对齐提供了新工具和方法，对推动人机交互的自然性和有效性具有重要意义。
why_it_matters_en: This research highlights fundamental misalignment between large language models and real user expectations, filling a gap in systematic analysis of user expectations. The proposed ExpectBench benchmark and LENS framework offer new tools and methods to improve model alignment with users, which is crucial for enhancing the naturalness and effectiveness of human-AI interaction.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: Expectation Alignment of Language Models for Real-World User Expectations. Category: research_papers. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.
ranking_reason: Selected from authoritative primary source; quality score 76.

title_zh: 面向多智能体系统的工作负载感知缓存策略
summary_zh: 多智能体系统通过将复杂任务分解为有向无环图（DAG）形式的专用智能体执行，天然适合在查询间缓存中间结果。现有缓存淘汰策略仅基于访问历史统一处理缓存条目，忽视了智能体执行环境中独特的结构和工作负载信号。本文提出了一种结合重计算成本、DAG依赖数量和智能体调用频率三种信号的工作负载感知淘汰策略，在内存受限情况下保留最有价值缓存条目。评测在三个不同复用场景的多智能体基准上，较无缓存基线延迟降低最高达64.7%，平均较次优有限容量策略延迟降低31.1%，且性能接近无限容量缓存，同时保证准确率不低于其他有限缓存方法。该策略可与计划级缓存和并行智能体执行等优化方法互补，针对多智能体流水线中不同效率瓶颈提升性能。
summary_en: Multi-agent systems break down complex tasks into directed acyclic graphs (DAGs) of specialized agent executions, allowing natural caching of intermediate results across queries. Existing eviction policies treat cached entries uniformly based on access history, ignoring structural and workload signals unique to agentic environments. This work introduces a workload-aware eviction policy combining recomputation cost, DAG dependency count, and agent invocation frequency into a unified score to retain the most valuable cache entries under memory constraints. Evaluations on three diverse multi-agent benchmarks show latency reductions up to 64.7% versus an uncached baseline and an average 31.1% improvement over the next best finite-capacity method, with performance approaching unbounded caching and maintaining equal or better accuracy. The workload-aware caching complements other agentic system optimizations like plan-level caching and parallel execution by addressing distinct efficiency bottlenecks in multi-agent pipelines.
why_it_matters_zh: 该研究提出的工作负载感知缓存策略充分利用多智能体系统特有的结构和执行信号，显著提升缓存效率和系统响应速度，尤其适合内存受限环境，推动多智能体系统在实际复杂任务中的性能优化。
why_it_matters_en: This research leverages unique structural and workload signals inherent to multi-agent systems to enhance caching efficiency and system responsiveness significantly, particularly under memory constraints, thereby advancing performance optimization of multi-agent systems in complex real-world tasks.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: Workload-Aware Caching for Multi-Agent Systems. Category: research_papers. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.
ranking_reason: Selected from authoritative primary source; quality score 77.

title_zh: TopoGuard：基于图论的RAG分割知识攻击防御方法
title_en: TopoGuard: Graph Theory Based Defenses Against Split-Knowledge Attacks on RAG
summary_zh: 本文介绍了TopoGuard，一种基于图论的方法，针对生产环境中检索增强生成（RAG）系统面临的分割知识攻击。该攻击通过组合多个单独无害的文档制造误导性关联，突破了现有的单文档过滤器如LlamaGuard的防护。TopoGuard构建检索文档的语义相似图，检测恶意拓扑结构，以识别攻击。实验证明TopoGuard在HotpotQA等数据集上比LlamaGuard召回率高21倍，并且具有低延迟和高鲁棒性。
summary_en: This paper introduces TopoGuard, a graph theory-based approach to defend against split-knowledge attacks on production Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) systems. The attack exploits the combination of individually benign documents to create false associations, bypassing existing per-document filters like LlamaGuard. TopoGuard builds semantic similarity graphs from retrieved documents and detects malicious topologies to identify attacks. Experiments demonstrate TopoGuard achieves 21 times higher recall than LlamaGuard on the HotpotQA dataset, while maintaining sub-millisecond latency and robustness against adaptive adversaries.
why_it_matters_zh: TopoGuard通过利用检索文档间的语义关系和图结构检测复杂攻击，突破了单文档防御的限制。它提升了RAG系统的安全性，有助于保障生成内容的可靠性，推动了RAG技术的安全应用。
why_it_matters_en: By leveraging semantic relationships and graph structures among retrieved documents, TopoGuard overcomes the limitations of per-document defenses. It enhances the security of RAG systems, ensuring more reliable generated content and advancing the safe deployment of RAG technologies.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: TopoGuard: Graph Theory Based Defenses Against Split-Knowledge Attacks on RAG. Category: research_papers. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.

title_zh: 语义场理论：历史起源、高阶交互与稳定语义推理
title_en: Semantic Field Theory: Historical Origin, Higher-Order Interaction, and Stabilized Semantic Inference
summary_zh: 语义场理论（SFT）起源于对语言游戏中强反形式主义解读的哲学批判，发展为一种用于词汇语义、高阶组合及稳定解释的计算模型。本文重构了该理论的发展历程，提出了适合计算语言学与表征学习独立评估的数学核心。核心观点是通过语义场表达的词汇表示、上下文变形、定义于词子集的交互项及语义能量动力学支配的稳定机制，构建可计算的语言组织层面。文章提供了五个形式元素：定义语义场模型元组结构，证明乘积场交互的高斯闭合性，使用莫比乌斯反演分解任意阶不可约交互，提出交互阶谱度量语义场质量分布，及通过能量泛函最小化实现稳定解释。示例展示了用高斯语义场表示“三词夏日”组合的Python实现和流程图。此成果不是自然语言意义的完整理论，也不替代语言的社会、语用或规范性解释。
summary_en: Semantic Field Theory (SFT) evolved from philosophical critiques of strong anti-formalist language game readings into a computational model for lexical semantics, higher-order composition, and stabilized interpretation. This paper reconstructs SFT's evolution and refines its mathematical core for computational linguistics and representation learning. It proposes modeling a tractable linguistic organization level via lexical representations as semantic fields, contextual deformation, interaction terms over token subsets, and stabilization via semantic energy dynamics. Five formal contributions include defining a semantic field model tuple, proving Gaussian product closure for multiplicative field interactions, generalizing the three-word problem using Mobius inversion to isolate irreducible semantic interactions at any order, introducing an order spectrum measuring field mass per interaction order, and formulating stabilized interpretation as energy minimization with conditions for existence and stability. A short example shows representing a three-word
why_it_matters_zh: 本研究深化了语义场理论的数学基础，提出了新颖的高阶交互分解方法及稳定解释框架，对计算语言学和语义表示学习具有重要意义。它为理解词汇语义的复杂组成和上下文依赖提供了理论和计算工具，推动自然语言处理领域的模型创新。
why_it_matters_en: This research advances the mathematical foundations of Semantic Field Theory by introducing novel high-order interaction decomposition and stabilized interpretation frameworks, which are significant for computational linguistics and semantic representation learning. It provides theoretical and computational tools to better understand complex lexical semantic composition and contextual dependence, fostering innovations in natural language processing models.

title_zh: MedGame：基于大型语言模型的医学教育故事化游戏化
title_en: MedGame: Storytelling Gamification Empowered by Large Language Models for Medical Education
summary_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）在医学教育中展示出潜力，但现有系统多聚焦于局部交互如问答或单轮反馈，而非将整个临床案例组织成决策导向的学习路径。我们提出MedGame框架，将静态临床案例转化为结构化、可执行的故事化游戏。MedGame采用双引擎设计：医学叙事设计师合成基于案例的临床故事线，包含状态和决策节点；故事导演将其转换为依赖感知的多模态编排计划，并通过发布的交互平台呈现。我们构建了包含5000案例的MedGame Bench基准和评测协议，用于医学叙事生成和故事导演。实验表明，针对任务的微调显著提升开源LLM在MedGame Bench上的表现，缩小与商业模型差距。初步学生研究显示，学习者认为MedGame比纯文本更具吸引力和实用性。
summary_en: Large Language Models (LLMs) demonstrate potential in medical education, yet most existing systems emphasize localized interactions like question answering or single-turn feedback instead of organizing full clinical cases into decision-centered learning trajectories. We introduce MedGame, a framework converting static clinical cases into structured, executable storytelling games. MedGame utilizes a dual-engine design: a Medical Narrative Designer synthesizing case-grounded clinical storylines with states and decision nodes, and a Story Director converting them into dependency-aware multimodal orchestration plans rendered on our interactive platform. We developed MedGame Bench, a 5,000-case benchmark and evaluation protocol for Medical Narrative Generation and Story Direction. Experiments show that task-specific fine-tuning significantly enhances open-source LLMs' performance on MedGame Bench, narrowing the gap with commercial models. A pilot student study indicates learners find MedGame more engaging and useful than text-only methods.
why_it_matters_zh: MedGame通过将临床教学案例故事化并游戏化，提升学习的沉浸感和决策训练效果，这种创新方法利用LLM生成复杂多节点临床故事，推动医学教育数字化转型。
why_it_matters_en: MedGame enhances medical education by gamifying clinical cases into immersive, decision-focused narratives. Leveraging LLMs to generate structured, interactive storytelling games advances digital medical education, making learning more engaging and effective.
